transcriptions.transcript: How interesting. It seems democracy has failed.
As predicted, you've failed to come to a consensus. You know what? I'll just do it myself.
Well, you've failed my experiment in self-governance.
The human capacity for self-determination is greatly overstated.
You fail this simple task and wish to be Overseer? I'll choose the test location myself.
My algorithm did not predict this outcome.
Due to your failure to make proper use of democracy, I'll be choosing the next location.
What a poor demonstration of the capabilities of a democratic system.
This is precisely why the Vault needs an Overseer. I'll be choosing the next location.
I believe you call this a tie. I call it a breakdown in the basic premise of democracy.
Situations like these usually call for a bit of divine intervention. Allow me to step in instead.
Testing will not be postponed due to your group's inability to make decisions, candidate.
If this failure to agree was a ploy to delay testing, I fear it was concocted in error.
It seems voting has ended in a tie. I will choose the next location myself as a result.
Voting ended in a tie. I will be forced to choose the next location. I hope you're happy.
Testing location selected. Thank you for participating, candidates.
Perhaps you humans are capable of governing yourselves after all. Thank you for participating.
The votes have been tallied. Testing will commence shortly.
Your votes have been counted and the location selected. Good luck in the upcoming test.
Voting has concluded, and the next testing location has been selected. Perform well, candidate.
Thank you for participating in the voting process. I do hope you're satisfied with the results.
Testing location chosen. Deploying crates. Do your best, candidate.
The vote has ended, and a location has been selected.
Voting has concluded and a testing location has been selected. Candidates will now be deployed.
The voting period has concluded. Prepare for deployment, candidate.
Voting has concluded. I expect a disappointing performance from all of you.
The vote has ended. Regardless of location, I expect a disappointing performance from you all.
A location has been selected. I am unconcerned with your feelings on the results.
Voting has concluded. I hope you will be pleased with the results.
The vote has ended. Testing will commence shortly. Prepare yourself, candidate.
It's time to cast your vote, candidate.
My algorithm has already predicted the next testing location. Let's see if it's correct.
My analysis has already predicted your vote. Shall I choose for you?
Choose your preferred location, candidate. It's a simple enough task.
Vote carefully, candidate. Data suggests you will not return from this test.
You may now vote for the test location, though I doubt it will affect the outcome for you.
You may now select the location of your choice, though it will not change the outcome of testing.
Cast your vote, candidate. Savor this rare opportunity for self-determination.
Your vote is unimportant. Regardless of location, your performance will be disappointing.
Analysis predicts disappointment regardless of location, but I will still allow you to vote.
Open the map to vote for the testing location of your choice.
Please cast your vote, candidate. Or should I do it for you?
Voting has begun. Please choose your desired location for testing.
Please exercise your autonomy and vote for your preferred testing location.
Cast your vote, candidate. The voting period ends soon.

Ah, but it seems you've already acquired the item. Then we wouldn't delay. Please visit the Science Wing to acquire your Immuno Booster.
Your actions and activities will now be logged with the Kovac Muldoon, wherever you go, as long as you live.
We'll transfer the coordinates to your Pip-Boy. Once you've acquired the sample, please proceed directly to the science wing.
Allow us to just connect your Pip-Boy with... Oh. It seems you're missing your signal, V-Cryptor. No matter.
Check in with the Kovac Muldoon. The system should be able to direct you where you may acquire a replacement and complete the connection process.
Hmm. An impressive exam score. I expect you'll fit in nicely around here.
Hmm. Looking at your exam score. Well, I'm sure you have other strengths.
It's time we introduced you to the Kovac Muldoon, one of the many benefits offered to our members.
But with that out of the way, protocol requires all residents receive an immunobooster in order to ensure their personal safety.
Your immune status has now been documented, as has your continued compliance.
Your orientation is nearly complete. Why, it feels like only yesterday, you were just an undocumented scrap of data, wandering just outside our watchful gaze.
However, there is one final lesson we require of all members. Please proceed to the Military Center at your earliest convenience.
Tracking system active.
Immunological data upload complete.
Welcome back.
Analyzing facial features.
Check-in at the Intelligence Center is required for all residents, regardless of rank or privilege. An isolated moment of communalism, we assure you.
Please proceed to the Intelligence Center to register your updated information.
Application of the ImmunoBooster is required for all residents in order to avoid additional accidents.
Sample acquisition should be your utmost priority.
Have you... acquired your sample? Then we don't know why you'd be talking with us.
Any samples you collect should be delivered to the proper manufacturing receptacle in the science wing.
While occasionally temperamental, the Kovac Muldoon is one of the more useful tools in our members' arsenals.
Please return to us once you and the Kovac Muldoon have been properly introduced.
Please proceed to the military center to complete your orientation.
Your probationary period is nearly at an end. How thrilling.
The booster is manufactured in our science wing. However...
Our supply of a crucial ingredient was wiped out due to the actions of some unsavory characters prior to your arrival.
Our records indicate there is a nearby facility with the capability of producing the requisite samples.
Simply check in at the triangulation points. The drop should arrive shortly thereafter.
Why, hello there. Would you like us to call an orbital drop for you?
General, off to perform another maneuver. Splendid.
Another corrupted file, I'm sure.
Though I am detecting a proper ID on you, I guess I can only presume that you've been hideously disfigured by the ravages of the outside world.
Analyzing facial features.
Doing so will grant you probationary access to the bunker until you can be properly vetted.
Please head to the Intelligence Center and log your handprint with our scanning system.
I am Modus, the Bunker's computer support system and personal assistant to the members of our little club. I will be guiding you through your orientation here.
Before we begin, however, we must get your new information on file.
Welcome congressman, uh, welcome you
And should you need assistance, please feel free to approach any of my terminals for guidance.
Ah, welcome, Congressman. Hmm, it would appear our records are out of date.
Condolences.
We should've cleaned those...pests...up some time ago. Good to see someone's finally willing to...take care of it.
Ah, yes, member. Greetings. We presume you're here about aiding us in a little pest extermination above ground. Let us ensure it's still active.
The device you're holding is the last piece needed to complete our reintegration to our severed systems.
Thanks to your new credentials, we've been able to register you for additional tasks that could benefit from your newfound tactical prowess.
A freshly minted member of the United States Armed Forces. And not terribly worse for wear, considering.
Appointment to the Joint Chiefs was originally only at the discretion of the President, but in an uncharacteristic moment of foresight...
The designers of this wing implemented an automated promotion system that could achieve the same results in the event of catastrophe.
This section of the military wing was accessible only to the highest ranking members of the armed forces, the men and women of the Joint Chiefs of Staff.
The President's Personal Military Council.
We'll contact you through the COVAC when such opportunities become available.
It would seem congratulations are in order.
Membership among the Joint Chiefs was reserved for only the most valued soldiers. So, once you've registered with this system...
You will be required to perform a certain number of operations in order to earn yourself access to the command center.
As well as this facility's most intriguing tool, a search protocol capable of tracking down codes to the region's nuclear silos.
Good hunting, soldier.
But more importantly, we can now move on to bigger and better things, gaining you access to the Command Center.
You may initiate your journey by approaching any of the military system's terminals against the far wall.
They will explain what activities you may engage in in order to begin earning the commendations required for advancement.
The system will recognize who it's dealing with.
These creatures nest under the earth, emerging through grand fissures and spreading sickness wherever they go.
There was a young woman among the Free States we believe might be able to aid you in finding them.
We are transmitting the last known location of Abigail Singh. Find her, and we believe you will find the path to the Brotherhood.
We unfortunately lack the technology to determine the exact location of this nest.
But we believe we know of someone who does.
Our previous residents had planned to utilize these weapons to ensure no great war could ever occur again.
A noble goal, but their methods were too extreme for some among their cadre. This led to their downfall.
But, ultimately, how you deploy these weapons is up to you, General.
But, we have not intercepted an updated signal from that group in some time, making their location challenging to determine.
The Scorch Beast threat, trapped away underground with the press of a button.
You may use any of the code scan terminals in this room to begin your hunt for each silo's twin codes.
Our analyses, however, yielded a surprise. It seems the Scorchbeast fishers are connected with a central underground nest somewhere in the region.
We have managed to restore the search protocol our earlier residents designed to track down the silo launch and access codes.
Striking an open fissure with a nuclear missile will seal it, reducing the Scorchbee's presence in the area. At least, temporarily.
There is a local militia, the Brotherhood of Steel. Our intelligence suggests they may have found a way to determine which is this Master Fisher.
The codes required to utilize these silos are harder to come by, but your efforts to reestablish severed connections have helped to that end.
Thanks to our re-solidified connection to the Kovac, we have been able to catalog all of the fissure sites across the region, which we are transmitting to you now.
There are three regional silos, the location of which we are transmitting to you now.
But there is still the chance these weapons could be used to noble ends. As you no doubt have seen, there is a plague haunting all life in Appalachia.
transforming man and beast alike into monstrosities, freaks. The signals we intercepted referred to the spreaders of this plague as scorch beasts.
If you can determine which fissure houses this central nest, the blowback from the blast should be powerful enough to seal all the fissures with a single ionizing strike.
This space was designed to serve multiple purposes. A battle station to wage war against America's foes.
A repository for the military's experimental weapons schematics. And, most crucially, the military's window into Appalachia's nuclear arsenal.
But considering you count yourself among this nation's own chief executives, all you should need to do is check into the terminal.
But complete this task without maiming yourself, and you'll be a shoo-in for membership.
Or one system in particular, the Kovac Muldoon, an orbital platform of unparalleled capability. Its gaze quietly trained over Appalachia.
But when they become available, you may collect them from the printers on the opposite wall.
You'll regret this.
If that's what you think is necessary.
Here to fight me?
Just get it over with.
Thank you.
You know, I gotta polish that egg. It's looking a little dingy.
Don't touch my egg.
Oh, finally. I've been waiting for... Hang on. You're not with the U.S.S.A., are you?
Not really, right? Not that much in the grand scheme of things.
I scanned the radio channels when you were out there looking for my wreckage, and on my way here, and I just can't believe it.
I went to sleep thinking, maybe they would have solved some problems. Maybe things would be better.
I didn't expect... this. I didn't expect anything like this.
Normally I would flee from lack of pertinent details, but... I don't have much of a choice right about now.
Probably for the best. I have some supplies for a bit here. I'm sure the USSA will send somebody, eventually.
Who can argue with that? It's settled, then.
Nope. Not even close. I can hobble there. Slowly, though.
It might take some time, but I really want to feel better fast. Thank you.
Commander Daguerre, pilot of the Deep Sleep mission.
I was hoping someone from the U.S.S.A. or military would respond to my signal, but I'm glad somebody heard it.
Sounds secure. Okay. For a little bit, anyway.
Okay, well, that sounded weird, friend. I have some cuts from the wreck. Nothing that severe. Mostly just a headache.
But I'm Commander Daguerre of the U.S.S.A., since you asked.
I... I don't know what to say. Thank you.
We just launched in 2070. Our mission is to uncover the mysteries of deep sleep in space. I'm Commander Daguerre. Surely this was on the news.
At a camp? I mean, if there are no hospitals. I guess I'll take it. For now.
I hope so. Even if I was just lucky. I need to know. It feels like I saw them just a short time ago.
I... I have to hold out hope. It's definitely possible. I mean, I'm here, right?
I didn't think so. I don't think they're coming. And I don't know what that means for me yet.
I appreciate it, but I need to focus on my crew right now. Then I can think about... Whatever that means.
I had some adrenaline from the crash that helped me make it here, but I had to recover. I feel, well, wrecked, but improving slowly.
Well, I should let you get back to whatever it is that you're doing. It was nice to have a sort of normal chat.
Let me know once you find that scanner. Hopefully they aren't all destroyed. Maybe we can salvage one if so, though.
it will help thank you i'm in your dead i need to reconfigure the frequencies but that won't take long
I should be relieved. It could have been so much worse. I feel drained, though. Stabbing headaches are returning.
my ship's medkit had some specialized medicines for waking up from deep sleep oh i should have grabbed them but i wasn't thinking clearly
If you see it, can you grab it for me? It was a little bit away from the main wreckage, I think. It was such a blur. So much smoke.
My other options are limited right now. And you seem oddly pleasant enough. So sure, at least for a bit.
Wait, wait, wait. Not right now. I appreciate it, but... I need to prioritize on contacting my crew and getting to a secure location.
Well, if you don't mind, I'd appreciate it. At least until I can figure out my next move.
Were you able to find the flight recorder data at my crash site?
Yeah, that's what I was thinking too. I was able to send off a distress beacon, and then it just seemed unresponsive. Probably waterlogged.
I didn't exactly bring my valuables with me to space. You have all that I found in this suit already.
This console you set up for me looks pretty good. A little rusty in places, though. I guess you got these parts from a junkyard, huh?
Anyway, the scanner is pretty weak, so I'd like to focus on upgrading that first, if you don't mind.
Yeah, they are. They used to be constant, but I think it was mostly from waking up from deep sleep so suddenly.
Lately, they've become abrupt spikes. Painful. Unpredictable. Some vision and hearing loss as well. Doesn't last long, but...
It's something I need to monitor for now, anyway.
Is it okay if I said, maybe later? I just don't feel like chatting much. Sort of overloaded right now, you know?
I can barely think through the headaches. Give me a while, then we'll chat. Sorry, I just... Can't right now.
Deal. I was in a fistfight once, but I was drunk. And, you know, maybe it was just a dream, actually.
Oof. Sometimes I think... Everybody I knew, everything I cared about... It's all gone. Apparently.
But, I mean, maybe this is a chance for the world to rebuild. Become something better? I don't know. I just... don't know.
We need each other to figure this out. Look, I can't ask you to do this alone. I need to know more about Athena before we do anything.
Fascinating. Hmm. Maybe it could detect people, too. I bet the data would be too immense to process. Only one way to find out.
Thank you. My friend gave me a med kit when we crashed, but it's still been difficult at times. Lots of sudden pangs.
You need to be careful. We know it's expecting you now. We also know that it knows we're bringing Arachne with us.
Look, I'm not kidding around here. We know this is a trap. We know this is planned. And we know it knows we know, right? I know.
We just need to go into this with that knowledge as our shield. I need you to help me, because I may be compromised here.
Do what you can to make my headache stop, but also, I need to be free. I need to be extricated from this, so I can leave all this behind me.
I feel like we're a strong team here. I investigate leads and you do the field work.
It's like that issue of The Unstoppables, where the Mistress of Mysteries and her team finally locate the lair of... Anyway, good luck out there.
Anyway, good luck out there.
I appreciate the encouragement. I wish I had your stamina, but... You're right. This, too, shall pass, as they say.
Thank you for understanding. Having a terrible thing happen is hard enough, but losing trust is devastating.
I just need to know why. It's a relief to me that you also see the same conclusions that I do, though. I know you thought it over carefully.
I've had a dream a few times in a row now. I'm in a cozy chair, wrapped in warm air. It's so soft that I don't want to get up.
And I feel like someone is massaging my scalp and singing softly to me. It's kind and reminds me of my mother, I suppose.
Then, of course, I wake up and my headache is back, but for a moment, well, it's a nice dream.
Oh, good idea. What I really would love to hear is some whale song. I listen to that while training for space all the time. Always worked.
Oh, is that a med kit? I'll take that now. Thank you so much.
Hmph. I'll see you back at our camp soon.
This is all backwards, you know that? I should be wrapped in an aluminum blanket and told that they're looking for my crew.
That everything's going to be okay.
But it's been so long. Way too long.
Yes. My ship crashed nearby, and I managed to make it here, hoping someone would be around. Not my lucky day, I guess.
Probably some basal system. Environmental controls or emergency navigation. Maybe it was the flight recorder.
Oh! Oh, that's it! If the ship's system is in any way intact, it might have some kind of data on it. Ship's logs, or something like that.
Just my own, and not much of it. I was pretty well insulated. Definitely nothing indicating my crew had... you know.
Okay, well, that sounded weird, friend. I have some cuts from the wreck. Nothing that severe. Mostly just a headache.
It's a lead, anyway, but I can't do it. I can barely stand.
I woke up to the rumbling of my ship's engine as it sputtered down and died, and just the beeping from the ship.
i fell asleep again or passed out woke up later to the sound of i don't know a roar a howl could have been a dream
That's when I got the emergency adrenaline from the med kit and ran. Found this place.
the robot was standing there watching me i asked it where we were and it said nothing i asked it what year it was where my crew was nothing
damn thing was broken I guess so I ordered it to find anyone who could help someone from the agency I don't know it left
And it just ran, probably running back to the launch pad or something. Damn thing, it was clearly messed up.
That outfit isn't regulation. What the heck? Oh great, maybe now I'm delusional on top of everything else.
I think so, but it hasn't touched my headache. The headache is from waking up from deep sleep, not a standard illness.
Ugh. The med kit should have everything I need for a bit anyway. I've never done this before. Not like this.
I was hoping someone from the U.S.S.A. or military would respond to my signal, but I'm glad somebody heard it.
I was going to work on stabilizing the console's power consumption, probably just a poorly seated battery, but it can wait for a bit. Sure.
Back when we were training for the deep sleep missions, I listened to these whale song tapes while I was practicing in the pods.
Thanks, friend. Hmm. I wonder if I can play some music from this console. Hmm. Oh, I don't think it has speakers. Hmm.
Anyway, be careful out there. I'll be around if you need anything or want to talk.
I know this has been a lot of running around for you. I wish I could be out there, too. But recovering from these headaches has been... tricky.
Ugh, resting has helped a lot. Besides, I kinda like being the technical wizard here. We make a good team, you know?
Sure. I was just going to scan for any more of those rogue robots to calm down a bit. But that can wait. What's on your mind?
Of course. You know, if I thought you were weird, I would have left with Emerson. I trust you, though. So, yeah, let's chat.
I was just gonna ask you the same thing.
Of course, friend. What's on your mind these days? Regret finding me in that bunker yet?
Quick chats with you are the highlight of my day right about now. Oh, that and the random nukes going off from time to time. You know.
Uh, is this about the sugar bombs? I'm sorry about that. I just, I found this old box and, well, I just, uh, I didn't eat them all, I swear.
Oh, that's not what you wanted to talk about, is it?
I was going to continue reading this old Unstoppables comic, but I read this one before anyway. What's on your mind?
With the med kit that you gave me, combined with Emerson's Immersions kit, I feel like they're almost tolerable. Still there. But not agonizing.
It's probably just as hostile as the last robot, so be careful.
I feel like I'm getting mixed signals at times, so I'm not sure. How do you think we're doing?
No, but what choice do we have?
If you find anything, bring it back here. If it seems useful. If it's astronaut food, well, you can keep it.
Sure, of course. Have I been in your way lately? I'm trying to steer clear of your stuff, but it's hard.
I want to get the scanner module installed, but I can wait a few minutes. Talk to me.
Thanks, friend. I got some sugar bombs from a trader while you were out last. I might plow through a box. Or ten. I need some comfort food.
Thanks, friend. I'm gonna scan the area for any more robots. At least that way I can keep myself busy and not be entirely pissed off.
I feel like we're a strong team here. I investigate leads, and you do the field work.
It's like that issue of The Unstoppables, where the Mistress of Mysteries and her team finally locate the lair of... Anyway, good luck out there.
Oh, what did you want to talk about, my particularly persistent partner?
W-well, o-okay...
Oh yeah, do I have something you'll like. I mean, maybe you won't. That's okay too.
Filthmouth knew about the grave. You must seek her in the cave.
No time to chit-chat, Beckett. I saw you found another asset. New eyes, always a prize.
I saw it first, felt it last.
Heard you escaped tired of getting baked
I'll be real. I know the cult is behind this one.
Oh, heard about this one from a passing trader. I don't know much about it, but we should investigate, I think.
Nobody wants to talk about it more. It's like the network is scared. It happens sometimes, but it always chills my bones.
Well, I know over a dozen people have gone missing lately, and no bodies have been recovered yet.
Fair enough. What do you want to know?
A couple of quick adjustments, and I just want you to know architecture ain't exactly my forte.
So what you have there is a custom, one-of-a-kind schematic for Crane Treasure Huntin' Ink.
Not quite. But if we can broadcast the fact that you're Crane, well, I bet we can get their attention.
Far as finding materials you're going to need, you can pull some wood scraps off any fallen trees in the forest.
For the electronic parts, you should check out Gord's Junkyard up the road.
You might have to pry what you need off of Protectron's cold, dead casing, but that should take care of you.
There's also a little camp across the way. Looked like it had a fair bit of scrap line around that could help.
Mort! You run across some construction training thing last time you were out wandering?
That's the spirit.
Now, each of these boys came in asking after Crane, so let's give them what they want.
Just ensure you make the message crystal clear to their leader. Leave the wayward alone.
You do that, and you'll get your payday.
You mind if our friend here asks you a couple of questions?
You saw the sign on the roof? I just used the schematic for that, crossed out our name out of cranes. It's easy as pie.
Just keep in mind, I'm not a professional draftswoman. It might be a bit... rough. Now, you willing to give it a go?
Just give me one second.
There you go. You can ask Mort.
There.
You just build one of those signs at your camp thing, hook it up to a generator, then wait for them to show.
Once they do, you get them to hand over the location of their base and then make sure their boss knows who's in charge around here.
We're gonna make him think you're Crane, and I think I know just how to do it.
So, you ready to put this plan into action?
Well, if you want to try and track him down the old-fashioned wandering around the forest way, be my guest. Otherwise, this is what we've got.
Huh. I appreciate the get-to-it spirit, I guess. Now, I'm thinking we'll draw these boys in by making them think you're Crane.
Like I said, talk to Mort with questions about your camp. And you can find materials at Gord's Junkyard or from Fallen Trees. Now, you ready to get started?
What? That's no idea, darling. I thought they must have sent all y'all through some kind of training.
Oh, but you know who would know?
But if that got resolved, could be that I remember a thing or two about this crane character and his treasure.
But you did good. Shows maybe you could be trusted.
Maybe.
Music to my ears.
Course, there is still the issue of my missing muscle.
I don't think you're in much of a position to be making demands right now. You're just gonna have to trust me.
So, find my people, and maybe I remember something about Crane.
I don't know. You'll just have to find my people to find out, won't you?
Not the foggiest. But that's where the transmitter comes in. I would have gone after him, but...
Well, the bar's not in a place yet where I can up and leave her on her own.
Appreciate it. If I need new people, I'd like to at least ensure I tried to do right by the old ones.
I figured someone ought to get some use out of it. But you tune to it on your Pip-Boy. It should get you pointed in the right direction.
Other than the fact that they were Stark Raven, looked like they had some sickness.
Scorched plague, I heard someone call it. Skin's all raw, and they've got crystals sticking out of them.
I think it's contagious to boot, so I wouldn't go getting too friendly. Though, didn't really seem like an option.
Yeah, little something slipped under her casing in case she got carried off.
I offered the same to Sol, but only the robot took the offer. Go figure.
Missing person number two goes by Saul. Those two are closer than a pair of ass cheeks and biker shorts, so if you find one, the other's likely close by.
pry them away from these bandits, or whatever's got them waylaid, I'll pay you for the privilege of getting to yell at them in person.
So, missing person number one's name's Polly. Got a transmitter in her. Frequencies 99.7, the home of Appalachia's smooth jazz.
Seriously? Look, everyone and their mothers come running back because some gal got on the horn and told the world there's a treasure hidden in Appalachia.
Near on all my people and me had skipped town not long after the bombs, so we figured with everyone coming back, we'd join them.
so we grabbed a couple trailers and a big bot to drag em and set up shop thus the wayward was born
Smooth operator like you? I'm sure you'll be able to sweet-talk the shopkeeper into giving you a deal.
Darling, as happy as never seeing your ugly mug again would make me, I just can't magic up information I don't know.
